title: drosera whittakeri var. praefolia
intro: Drosera whittakeri var. praefolia, also known as the sundew plant, is a carnivorous plant species that belongs to the Droseraceae family and is native to New Zealand. The medicinal properties of Drosera whittakeri var. praefolia include its potential in treating respiratory issues, coughs, and colds due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of the plant are attributed to its ability to soothe and calm the body, providing relief from stress and anxiety, and its herbal preparations are traditionally used as a natural remedy for respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of Drosera whittakeri var. praefolia include tinctures, teas, and infusions, which can be consumed orally to reap its medicinal benefits.
